corrections.text: The article attributes the quotation "provided this data through a Bluesky thread analyzing May 2026 gaming market trends" to ComicBook. ComicBook's article actually reads that Piscatella "recently shared an in-depth breakdown of U.S. gaming market trends for May 2026" and refers to "this Bluesky thread." The substance (a Bluesky thread covering May 2026 trends) is accurate, but the wording inside the quotation marks is a paraphrase, not a verbatim quote.

The article attributes the quotation "Only the Game Boy Advance exceeded it, with 6.5 million units" to Nintendo Life. Nintendo Life's article reads: 'Only the Game Boy Advance, shifting 6.5 million units, "exceeds it".' The directly quoted fragment in the source is only "exceeds it" (present tense); the figure of 6.5 million units is the source's own paraphrase rather than part of the quoted text. The underlying fact is correct and is independently corroborated by ComicBook, which reports the Game Boy Advance sold 6.5 million units after its 2001 U.S. launch.
